tools_enabled: local deterministic content and hash validation

validation_gates.reason: Repository identity, public visibility, authenticated admin/push permission, target path, immutable base, target blob, review-branch route, API_ONLY_STRICT profile, two-file change budget, acceptance criteria, and stop conditions were resolved before mutation.

Main advanced from 8b016972c1509d2f618cf72ce259e925ec537d0a to 7af05fc50e5a1bab28b314532b0a66d2839a229b through unrelated merged work. The target retained blob 7807275a0afca4aa1518787a392225e2732a6757 and the branch was created from the newer base.

Both Directory Rules copies, proposed schema-home ADR, drift register, validator parent/indexes, source registry, CODEOWNERS, PR contract, workflow documentation, domain workflow, and path-scoped AGENTS.md chain were checked. The document, schema, slug, and registry conflicts are surfaced rather than resolved.

The existing doc_id and created date were preserved, v0.2 explicitly supersedes v0.1, and the generic parent remains subordinate to semantic contracts, schemas, policy, evidence, tests, release records, and accepted child validators.

Direct probes and repository reads established a README-only generic lane, absent expected executable/test/schema paths, a working shared runtime not registered for crossings, README-only child/domain/topology lanes, a draft contract, compatibility/index-only schema surfaces, greenfield policy, documentation-led tests, and TODO-only domain workflow.

The README defines a thin generic parent, delegates Bridge/RiverCrossing, facility/topology, shared geometry/freshness, evidence, policy, release, and domain-owned truth, and prevents child specialization or graph projection from becoming Crossing authority.

The README contains no current route conditions, operational instructions, exact sensitive locations, restricted infrastructure details, private access records, or source payloads. It explicitly denies live routing, legal access, structural/load, water/flood, safe-passage, rail-operating, and emergency authority.

The relevant domain workflow uses pull_request/main-push triggers, ubuntu-latest, a major-tag checkout action, and TODO-only echo jobs. Workflow documentation records incomplete token, branch-protection, required-check, and immutable-pinning evidence; these gaps remain NEEDS VERIFICATION and no workflow is treated as publication authority.

The final remote README has one H1, unique headings, explicit unique anchors, resolved internal navigation, balanced fences, parseable KFM Meta Block YAML, no trailing whitespace, and a final newline.
All eighteen repository-relative links added to the README were verified through GitHub reads at the pinned base.

GitHub returned README blob 82e9ab67c5e784814811684b4b9ffac7a283c0db on commit 30e46c9d80ce2fb19fb5e364ac5855b3d8c01e30. The locally reconstructed canonical bytes reproduce that exact Git blob and SHA-256 3f2b35d0d311fa15c6e89da532fc73f63a4d0b450dd68b563ede08bff4ba6bfc; pre-receipt scope contains exactly the target README.

API_ONLY_STRICT was selected. The repository was not cloned and repository-native commands were not executed locally. Pull-request workflow state will be inspected after PR creation and pending or unavailable checks will not be reported as passing.

notes: AI-authored documentation-only revision on a scoped API-only review branch. The README replaces speculative parent guidance with current repository evidence, preserves the generic-parent/child/domain boundaries, and adds implementation acceptance, validation, security, correction, and rollback contracts without creating executable or publication authority. This receipt is process provenance only; it is not factual proof, policy permission, human approval, release authority, or publication authority.
